title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. alpina
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. alpina, also known as the alpine columbine, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of alpine columbine include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Alpine columbine can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in homeopathic remedies to promote overall well-being.
